title: GIMS Greater Noida expands paediatric orthopaedic surgery capabilities with support from Healthium MedTech and Plan India
summary: Institute equipped to undertake surgeries for clubfoot, developmental dysplasia of the hip, limb deformities and complex bone disorders
thbAction: Pitch THB's diagnostics CRM to help labs manage doctor outreach, collection-center expansion, and B2B referral relationships while improving conversion and retention.
contentHtml:
Institute equipped to undertake surgeries for clubfoot, developmental dysplasia of the hip, limb deformities and complex bone disorders

Government Institute of Medical Sciences, with support from Healthium MedTech’s CSR initiative and implemented by Plan International India Chapter, has expanded its paediatric orthopaedic surgery capabilities. The institute is now equipped to undertake surgeries for conditions including developmental dysplasia of the hip (DDH), clubfoot, limb deformities, fractures and complex bone disorders.

The institute has installed equipment including the Midas Rex MR8 High Speed Electric Drill System, Paediatric Hip Plating Set, Paediatric and Small Bone Drill & Saw System, Paediatric Orthopaedic Instrument Set and a C-Arm Machine. According to the organisations involved, the equipment will support paediatric orthopaedic surgical care at the institute.

India continues to face a burden of paediatric orthopaedic conditions, with 1 to 2 per cent of children born with disorders such as clubfoot, while other deformities arise from trauma, infections or untreated conditions. In Uttar Pradesh, limited access to specialised care often results in delayed treatment, leading to complications, disability and higher treatment costs.

The organisations stated that the upgraded infrastructure at GIMS is expected to support treatment for more than 1,000 children annually and reduce referrals outside the region.

Dr G.N. Singh, Adviser to the Hon’ble Chief Minister, Uttar Pradesh, said, “This initiative reflects our commitment to ensuring that every child, regardless of background, has access to quality and timely healthcare. Strengthening public health institutions through such partnerships is essential to building an inclusive and equitable healthcare system.”

Dr Rakesh Kumar Gupta, Director General, GIMS, said, “With this advanced equipment, GIMS is now prepared to handle complex paediatric orthopaedic cases with greater precision and safety. This will significantly reduce referrals and ensure that children receive timely treatment closer to home.”

Anish Bafna, CEO and MD, Healthium MedTech, said, “At Healthium, we are focused on improving clinical outcomes by enabling both innovation and access. Supporting initiatives such as the GIMS and Plan International India Chapter project reflects our effort to strengthen care delivery systems and equip healthcare providers with the tools needed to deliver specialised paediatric orthopaedic care. By enhancing on-ground capabilities, we aim to ensure that timely, reliable treatment reaches children who need it the most.”

Mohammed Asif, Executive Director, Plan International India Chapter, said, “Through this partnership, we are strengthening access to specialised paediatric healthcare for vulnerable communities. This intervention will directly benefit over 1,000 children every year, ensuring they receive timely, safe and life-changing orthopaedic treatment without financial or geographic barriers.”

He also highlighted the role of public-private partnerships in expanding access to specialised healthcare services for underserved populations.

The launch event included an inauguration ceremony, screening of an impact film, addresses by dignitaries, beneficiary testimonials and the formal handover of equipment to the GIMS orthopaedics department.

According to the organisations, the initiative is expected to strengthen GIMS’ role in paediatric orthopaedic care in western Uttar Pradesh by improving access to treatment for children in the region.
